data.current_description: Applies a partial update to an existing Response Policy Rule.
data.summary_suggestion: Partially update a Response Policy Rule
data.description_suggestion: Applies a partial update to an existing Response Policy Rule within a Response Policy. Only the fields provided in the request body will be modified; omitted fields retain their current values. Returns the updated ResponsePolicyRule object. This operation modifies the DNS query behavior for the affected Response Policy.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity on what fields can be updated, what is returned, and the side effects on DNS query behavior. Added details about partial update semantics, return value, and operational impact for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Updates an existing Response Policy Rule.
data.summary_suggestion: Replace an existing Response Policy Rule
data.description_suggestion: Replaces an existing Response Policy Rule within a Response Policy with the provided configuration. This is a full replacement operation - all fields must be specified in the request body. Returns the updated ResponsePolicyRule object. Changes take effect immediately and modify DNS query behavior for the affected Response Policy.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is too vague about update semantics (full replacement vs. partial), what must be provided, what is returned, and side effects. data.current_description: Gets the access control policy for a resource. Returns an empty policy if the resource exists and does not have a policy set.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for a ManagedZone resource. Returns the current GoogleIamV1Policy object containing role bindings and permissions. If the resource exists but has no policy set, returns an empty policy. Requires the appropriate IAM permission to view policies.

data.current_description: Sets the access control policy on the specified resource. Replaces any existing policy. Can return `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, and `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors.
data.description_suggestion: Sets the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for a ManagedZone resource, replacing any existing policy. Requires a GoogleIamV1SetIamPolicyRequest containing the new policy bindings. Returns the updated GoogleIamV1Policy object. May return NOT_FOUND, INVALID_ARGUMENT, or PERMISSION_DENIED errors. Requires appropriate IAM permissions to modify policies.

data.current_description: Returns permissions that a caller has on the specified resource. If the resource does not exist, this returns an empty set of permissions, not a `NOT_FOUND` error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.
data.summary_suggestion: Test caller's IAM permissions on resource
data.description_suggestion: Tests which Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions the authenticated caller has on the specified ManagedZone resource. Accepts a GoogleIamV1TestIamPermissionsRequest containing a list of permissions to check. Returns a list of permissions the caller actually has. If the resource does not exist, returns an empty permission set rather than a NOT_FOUND error. Designed for building permission-aware UIs and tools, not for authorization enforcement. May fail open without warning.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity on what input is required and what is returned. Added details about request/response schemas and clarified the operation's intended use case for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Creates a new ResourceRecordSet.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new resource record set
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new ResourceRecordSet within a specified managed zone. Accepts a ResourceRecordSet object defining the DNS record details (name, type, TTL, and rdata). data.current_description: Deletes a previously created ResourceRecordSet.
